title: Goeppertia reginae
taxonomy: Goeppertia reginae is classified under the family Marantaceae. This family consists of around 31 genera, including Goeppertia. Goeppertia reginae is further classified under the genus Goeppertia, which is a part of the order Zingiberales. The order Zingiberales is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the ginger order. Goeppertia reginae is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.

intro: Goeppertia reginae, also known as the Queen of the Night, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of South America, belonging to the Arecaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as wound healing, fever reduction, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Goeppertia reginae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
